ocr.text: The data presented in the accompanying table offers a comprehensive overview of the relationship between BaP and PCB118
exposure and various metabolic markers across different species at Time 1. This investigation is particularly focused on the
influence of specific SNOMED CT concepts, namely “Abnormal body temperature (finding)” and “Ineffective
thermoregulation (finding),” which serve as critical indicators of metabolic dysregulation in response to environmental
contaminants.
Treatment and Time  Synergism of BaP and PCB  Antagonism of BaP and PCB

123,979,008 Abnormal body temperature (finding)  85,623,003 Ineffective thermoregulation (finding)
248,062,006 Self-injurious behaviour (finding)  130,968,006 Self-mutilation (finding)
284,905,001 Difficulty performing toileting activities (finding)  284,905,001 Self-toileting deficit (finding)
247,592,009 Poor short-term memory (finding)|  423,698,005 Limited recall of recent event (finding)

RWI  DF  SS  MS  F  P
The table delineates both included and excluded SNOMED CT concepts, highlighting the methodological rigor in selecting

relevant clinical findings for analysis. 

Furthermore, the metrics of RWI (Relative Weight Index) and DF (Dysfunction Factor)
are employed to quantify the degree of metabolic disturbance observed in subjects exposed to these compounds. The inclusion
of adipokines such as Adiponectin and Leptin, along with fatty acid-binding proteins (FABP-4) and enzymes like HSL
(hormone-sensitive lipase), underscores the multifaceted nature of metabolic responses. By focusing on these parameters, the
study elucidates the potential pathways through which BaP and PCB118 may modulate metabolic homeostasis, thereby
contributing to a more profound understanding of their health implications. The findings presented herein are essential for
informing future research directions and public health policies.
